docs.title: Azure Functions OpenAPI
docs.text: Welcome to azure-functions-openapi — a library that provides seamless integration of OpenAPI (Swagger) documentation for Python-based Azure Functions.

docs.title: ✨ Features
docs.text:
• @openapi decorator with summary, description, tags, request_model, response_model
  • Automatic generation of:
  • /openapi.json
  • /openapi.yaml
  • /docs (Swagger UI)
  • Pydantic-based schema validation and generation
  • Lightweight and fully compatible with Azure Functions Python Programming Model v2

docs.title: 🚀 Getting Started
docs.text:
Install the package:

pip install azure-functions-openapi

Decorate your function:

@openapi(
    summary="Say Hello",
    description="Returns a greeting message.",
    response_model=HelloResponse
)
def hello(req: HttpRequest) -> HttpResponse:
    ...

docs.title: ✅ M1. Project Setup
docs.text:
• [x] Project scaffold with src/, tests/, and examples/ directories
  • [x] Configure pyproject.toml, Makefile, .editorconfig, .gitignore
  • [x] Set Python version to >=3.8
  • [x] Use hatch for versioning
  • [x] Setup uv for editable install
  • [x] Add .pre-commit-config.yaml and basic hooks

docs.title: ✅ M2. Core MVP Features (v0.2.0)
docs.text:
• [x] @openapi decorator: capture summary, description, response
  • [x] Central registry for function metadata
  • [x] /openapi.json route for OpenAPI spec generation
  • [x] /docs (/swagger) route to serve Swagger UI
  • [x] Create example Azure Function

docs.title: ⬜ M3. Extended Features (v0.3.0)
docs.text:
• [x] Inference of HTTP method and route path from Azure trigger
  • [x] Support for parameters (query/path/header)
  • [x] Support for requestBody specification
  • [x] Define response schemas (e.g. JSON schema, examples)
  • [x] Integrate with pydantic or type hints
  • [x] Custom tags, operationId
  • [x] Markdown description support
  • [x] Parameter type expansion: cookie (remaining type)
  • [x] YAML output (/openapi.yaml endpoint)

docs.title: ⬜ M4. Quality & CI (v0.4.0)
docs.text:
• [x] Makefile targets: format, lint, typecheck, test, check
  • [x] pre-commit hooks (black, ruff, mypy, etc.)
  • [x] Add pytest unit tests for decorators and generator
  • [ ] Integrate GitHub Actions CI workflow
  • [ ] Measure test coverage and add badge

docs.title: ⬜ M5. Release & Documentation
docs.text:
• [x] hatch build for packaging
  • [x] Add README.md usage examples and basic documentation
  • [x] Add docs/ folder with index.md, usage.md, contributing.md
  • [x] Add mkdocs.yml for structured site docs
  • [ ] Add Swagger UI screenshot/preview
  • [ ] Add CHANGELOG generator with git-changelog
  • [ ] Publish first release to PyPI
  • [ ] Improve Swagger UI rendering (examples, schema preview, rich formatting)
  • [ ] (Optional) Host demo of Swagger UI (e.g., GitHub Pages or Azure Static Web App)

docs.title: ⬜ M6. OpenAPI 3.1 & Compatibility (v0.4.0+)
docs.text:
• [ ] OpenAPI 3.1 support (e.g., $schema, JSON Schema 2020-12)
  • [ ] OpenAPI version selection (3.0 / 3.1 toggle)

docs.title: @openapi Decorator
docs.text: This library provides an @openapi decorator that adds OpenAPI metadata to Azure Function routes.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
Name Type Description summary str Short summary shown in Swagger UI description str Long description in Markdown request_model BaseModel Pydantic model for request body response_model BaseModel Pydantic model for response body tags List[str] Tags for grouping endpoints operation_id str Unique identifier for the operation

Example usage:

@openapi(
    summary="Create a new item",
    description="Creates a new item and returns it.",
    request_model=ItemRequest,
    response_model=ItemResponse,
    tags=["Items"]
)

docs.title: [0.1.0] - Initial Release
docs.text:
• @openapi decorator
  • OpenAPI JSON/YAML route generators
  • Swagger UI support
  • Pydantic model integration

docs.title: Contributing Guide
docs.text: We welcome contributions to the azure-functions-openapi project!

docs.title: How to Contribute
docs.text:
1. Fork the Repository

  2. Create a New Branch 

    git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name

  3. Write Code & Tests

  4. Run make test to ensure everything passes.

  5. Follow code style using black, ruff, and mypy.

  6. Commit Your Changes 

    git commit -m "feat: describe your feature"

  7. Push and Create a Pull Request

docs.title: Project Commands
docs.text:
make format      # Format code with black
make lint        # Lint with ruff
make typecheck   # Type check with mypy
make test        # Run tests

docs.title: Code of Conduct
docs.text: Be respectful and inclusive. See the Contributor Covenant for details.

docs.title: Development Guide
docs.text: This document provides guidance for setting up the development environment for the azure-functions-openapi project.

docs.title: Python Version
docs.text:
• This project currently uses Python 3.8.
  • If you upgrade to Python 3.9+, you may need to update pre-commit hook versions accordingly.

docs.title: Local Setup
docs.text:
Clone and install the project in editable mode:

git clone https://github.com/yeongseon/azure-functions-openapi.git
cd azure-functions-openapi
pip install -e .

Then install development dependencies and pre-commit hooks:

make setup
pre-commit install

docs.title: Pre-commit Hooks
docs.text: This project uses pre-commit to enforce code quality standards before each commit.

docs.title: Python 3.8 Compatibility
docs.text:
The following versions are pinned in .pre-commit-config.yaml:

Tool Version Reason black 23.11.0 >=24.x requires Python 3.9+ bandit 1.7.7 >=1.8.x requires Python 3.9+

If using Python 3.9+, you may run:

pre-commit autoupdate

Warning: Revert to compatible versions above if using Python 3.8.

docs.title: Common Commands
docs.text:
pre-commit run --all-files   # Run hooks on all files
pre-commit clean             # Reset hook environments

docs.title: Development Commands
docs.text:
Use the following Make targets:

make setup       # Install dev dependencies
make format      # Run black
make lint        # Run ruff
make typecheck   # Run mypy
make test        # Run pytest

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
azure-functions-openapi/
├── src/
├── tests/
├── examples/
├── docs/
│   └── development.md
├── .pre-commit-config.yaml
└── pyproject.toml

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• If you encounter errors related to Python version compatibility, check .pre-commit-config.yaml
  • Ensure your virtual environment matches the Python version constraints

docs.title: Documentation Site with MkDocs
docs.text: This project uses MkDocs and the Material for MkDocs theme to build and deploy its documentation site.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
To build and serve the documentation locally, install the following:

pip install mkdocs mkdocs-material

docs.title: Local Preview
docs.text:
To run the documentation site locally:

mkdocs serve

Visit http://localhost:8000 in your browser to preview the documentation.

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
Documentation is deployed to GitHub Pages automatically using GitHub Actions.

The workflow file is located at:

.github/workflows/deploy.yml

docs.title: Deployment Trigger
docs.text:
The workflow runs on every push to the main branch and executes the following command:

mkdocs gh-deploy --force

This publishes the site to the gh-pages branch.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
The documentation structure and appearance are defined in mkdocs.yml. It includes:

  • Site name and theme
  • Navigation menu
  • Markdown extensions
  • Repository and edit links

You can customize these settings as needed.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Always run mkdocs serve before committing to validate changes.
  • Use mkdocs build to generate the static site without serving.
  • Ensure all Markdown files are included in navigation in mkdocs.yml.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: This project supports Python-based Azure Functions using the Programming Model v2.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.8+
  • Azure Functions Core Tools
  • Azure Functions Python Programming Model v2

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install azure-functions-openapi

Make sure your requirements.txt includes:

azure-functions
azure-functions-openapi

docs.title: Usage Guide: azure-functions-openapi
docs.text: This guide walks you through documenting your Python Azure Functions with OpenAPI (Swagger) using azure-functions-openapi.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
pip install azure-functions-openapi

For the development version:

git clone https://github.com/yeongseon/azure-functions-openapi.git
cd azure-functions-openapi
pip install -e .

docs.title: Folder Snapshot
docs.text:
azure-functions-openapi/
├── src/
├── tests/
├── examples/
└── docs/

docs.title: 2. @openapi Decorator
docs.text: Annotate each function with @openapi and the library will register metadata for the spec builder.

docs.title: Supported Arguments
docs.text: Argument Type Description summary str One‑line summary description str Markdown description tags list[str] Tag list to group the endpoint operation_id str Custom unique identifier route str Override default path (e.g. /users/{id}) method str HTTP verb (GET, POST, PUT, …) parameters list Query, path, header or cookie parameters request_model BaseModel Pydantic model → requestBody schema request_body dict Manual requestBody schema (use when not relying on a model) response_model BaseModel Pydantic model → 200‑response schema response dict Manual responses keyed by HTTP status code

docs.title: 3. Models with Pydantic (v2 only)
docs.text:
Note azure-functions-openapi targets Pydantic v2. Older v1 models are not supported.

from pydantic import BaseModel

class RequestModel(BaseModel):
    username: str
    age: int

class ResponseModel(BaseModel):
    message: str

These classes are converted into JSON‑schema and injected under components/schemas.

docs.title: 4. Parameters (query / path / header / cookie)
docs.text:
parameters = [
    {
        "name": "q",
        "in": "query",  # path, header, or cookie also allowed
        "required": False,
        "schema": {"type": "string"},
        "description": "Search query string",
    }
]

All types except cookie are fully supported today (cookie support is on the roadmap).

docs.title: 5. OpenAPI 3.0 vs 3.1
docs.text: Status Notes 3.0 ✔ Current output 3.1 ⏳ Planned: nullable, oneOf/anyOf/const, JSON Schema 2020‑12

docs.title: 6. Docs (Swagger UI) Customization
docs.text:
Override swagger_ui() in your project to inject custom assets:

<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://example.com/custom.css">
<script src="https://example.com/custom.js"></script>

Inside the HTML you can tweak the SwaggerUIBundle({ ... }) options.

docs.title: 7. JSON & YAML Endpoints
docs.text:
Endpoint Purpose /docs Interactive Swagger UI /openapi.json Raw spec (JSON) /openapi.yaml Raw spec (YAML, requires PyYAML)

These outputs import directly into Postman, SwaggerHub, Azure API Management, etc.

docs.title: Example Function
docs.text:
@app.route(route="http_trigger", auth_level=func.AuthLevel.ANONYMOUS)
@openapi(
    summary="Greet user",
    description="""Returns a greeting.

### Usage

Pass `name` in query or body.""",
    request_model=RequestModel,
    response_model=ResponseModel,
    tags=["Example"],
    operation_id="greetUser",
)
def http_trigger(req: func.HttpRequest) -> func.HttpResponse:
    ...

Happy documenting! 🚀

docs.title: Hello OpenAPI Example
docs.text: The hello_openapi example demonstrates the most minimal usage of the @openapi decorator.

docs.title: File
docs.text: • examples/hello_openapi/function_app.py

docs.title: Key Concepts
docs.text:
• One route
  • No request model
  • Returns a simple greeting response

docs.title: Sample
docs.text:
@app.route(route="hello", ...)
@openapi(summary="Hello", description="Returns a greeting.", response_model=HelloResponse)
def hello(req: HttpRequest) -> HttpResponse:
    ...

docs.title: Todo API Example
docs.text:
The todo_api example shows how to build a basic Todo API using:

  • Azure Functions (v2 model)
  • @openapi decorators
  • Pydantic models

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• POST /create_todo to add a new item
  • GET /list_todos to fetch all items

docs.title: Directory
docs.text:
• examples/todo_api/function_app.py
  • examples/todo_api/host.json
  • examples/todo_api/local.settings.json
